title: Lebanon War, 1982
author_or_org: PalQuest / Interactive Encyclopedia of the Palestine Question
geographic_scope: Lebanon-wide with emphasis on invasion and Beirut
strengths: Compact synthesis of key force-size and death figures; useful for explaining why 1982 must be further split.
limitations: Not a named-victim or incident database; mixed-status totals not sufficient for DR.
notes: Used mainly to justify non-estimability of a single 1982 episode.

title: Civilian Pawns: Laws of War Violations and the Use of Weapons on the Israel-Lebanon Border
author_or_org: Human Rights Watch / Human Rights Watch Arms Project / Human Rights Watch Middle East
date_scope: Primarily 1993 Operation Accountability, with broader border-war context
strengths: Detailed, conflict-specific human rights investigation with multiple contemporaneous death claims and displacement evidence.
limitations: Combatant force-exposure denominator remains weak; some totals are mixed-status or sourced from officials with incentives.
notes: Core numerator source for the 1993 partial review.

title: Operation Accountability
strengths: Clear official dates and Israeli-side casualty counts.
limitations: Partisan on Hezbollah death counts; not a civilian-protection audit.
notes: Used as a bounding source rather than sole truth.

title: Israel/Lebanon: Operation "Grapes of Wrath"
strengths: Concise direct-death and injury splits with displacement totals for both sides.
limitations: Hezbollah death count remains contested; denominator evidence is still poor.
notes: Core numerator source for the 1996 partial review.

title: Why They Died: Civilian Casualties in Lebanon during the 2006 War with Israel
strengths: Best open-source structured split between civilians and combatants on the Lebanese side.
limitations: Not a full named-victim registry for every death; some additional official deaths remain outside its explicit civilian/combatant split.
notes: Primary numerator source for Israeli-inflicted deaths in Lebanon in 2006.

title: Civilians under Assault: Hezbollah's Rocket Attacks on Israel in the 2006 War
strengths: Specific and side-attributed on Hezbollah rocket effects inside Israel.
limitations: Soldier deaths here are only those inside Israel, not total IDF war deaths.
notes: Used for home-front harm, but not as the sole source for total Israeli military deaths.

title: Report of the Secretary-General on the implementation of Security Council resolution 1701 (2006)
strengths: Authoritative official cross-border totals in one place.
limitations: Lebanese-side totals are not fully status-split; Israeli military deaths are total war deaths rather than only home-front deaths.
notes: Used to anchor official total deaths and displacement on both sides.

title: Report of the Commission of Inquiry on Lebanon pursuant to Human Rights Council resolution S-2/1
author_or_org: United Nations Human Rights Council Commission of Inquiry on Lebanon
variables_supported: belligerent structure (Israel vs Hezbollah; LAF not active)
strengths: Excellent for geometry, displacement, and theater-definition notes.
limitations: Not a full combatant/civilian death registry.
notes: Key denominator-context source for the 2006 full estimate.

title: Hard Fighting: Israel in Lebanon and Gaza
strengths: Useful secondary synthesis for bounding Hezbollah force size and fatality disagreement.
limitations: Synthesizes other sources rather than creating new victim-level data.
notes: Used to widen intervals rather than replace primary sources.

title: We Were Caught Unprepared: The 2006 Hezbollah-Israeli War
variables_supported: IDF troops in southern Lebanon by August 5
Hezbollah force south of the Litani
Israeli high claims for Hezbollah deaths
strengths: Useful operational detail on troop concentrations and the south-Litani battle space.
limitations: Not a civilian casualty study; some casualty estimates are explicitly uncertain or likely exaggerated.
notes: Supports the low Hezbollah force scenario and lower-bound IDF in-theater troop presence.

citation_short: RAND, Air Operations in Israel's War Against Hezbollah
title: Air Operations in Israel's War Against Hezbollah: Learning from Lebanon and Getting It Right in Gaza
variables_supported: IDF troop surge to around 30,000 in Lebanon
Israeli assessment of Hezbollah active force around 2,000-3,000 at one point
strengths: Strong operational detail on force deployments and surge timing.
limitations: Military-operational emphasis; not a mortality study.
notes: Used to structure the high-end IDF denominator bound and contextual Hezbollah force estimates.

title: The Second Lebanon War: Strategic Perspectives
author_or_org: Institute for National Security Studies (eds. Shlomo Brom and Meir Elran)
variables_supported: description of simultaneous military and civilian fronts
strengths: Useful contextual support for the two-front geometry of the 2006 war.
notes: Supports the idea that 2006 combined civilian and military fronts on both sides.

title: Israel Under Rocket Attack: A Profile of Displacement and Destruction
author_or_org: Jacob Blaustein Institute for the Advancement of Human Rights
strengths: One of the few compiled sources for exposed civilian population in northern Israel.
limitations: Some figures are themselves compilations from Reuters or official spokespeople.
notes: Primary civilian denominator-context source for Hezbollah-inflicted deaths in 2006.

title: World Report 2025: Lebanon
variables_supported: Israeli civilian deaths from Hezbollah munitions
strengths: Recent high-quality synthesis with subgroup counts and displacement.
limitations: Not a combatant/civilian full-status registry; relies partly on ministry totals.
notes: Used to document the scale of the 2023-2024 episode while explaining why DR remains unestimated.

title: Q&A: Behind the data on the Israel-Hezbollah war
pages_or_sections: sections on civilian impact and ground operation
variables_supported: ground-operation geography in Bint Jubayl, Marjayun, and Tyre districts
strengths: Directly states the current obstacle to estimation: lack of clarity on militant vs civilian fatalities.
limitations: Not itself a final fatality registry.
notes: Key non-estimability source for the 2023-2024 episode.

title: Israel-Hezbollah truce holds, displaced Lebanese begin to journey home
strengths: Clear episode boundary and late-war aggregate totals.
limitations: Journalistic aggregate reporting; status splits remain incomplete.
notes: Used to set the end date of the 2023-2024 episode.

title: Israel's Hezbollah attacks are likely to continue beyond Iran war, source says
strengths: Early contemporaneous boundary-setting for the renewed war.
notes: Used to set the start of the 2026 episode at 2026-03-02.

title: Israel warns Lebanon of 'disastrous repercussions' as week's death toll nears 300
author_or_org: Reuters (Maya Gebeily, Laila Bassam, and Maayan Lubell)
strengths: Adds military-status detail (three troops) and displacement context.
limitations: Aggregate and fast-moving; not a final casualty classification source.

title: Israel says it targeted Iranian commanders in Beirut as death toll nears 400
variables_supported: Israeli claim of Hezbollah militants killed
strengths: Most up-to-date research-cutoff aggregate for the renewed 2026 war window.
limitations: Health ministry still does not distinguish civilians from military personnel.
notes: Used only to bound the open 2026 episode, not to produce a DR.
